commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=952ca9737c2f41a2db0b62fba9ea85285c144a85)
commands.review_summary:
The branch stores CLI parse argv in program context, resolves it through command ancestors during lazy reparse, passes rewritten argv into `buildProgram`, and adds focused program-context/reparse tests.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +51. Total +76 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: GitHub check annotations on the PR head show the readonly argv type error and lazy CLI smoke failures in `program.smoke.test.ts`. I did not run tests locally because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The code defects are narrow, but this external PR also needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof, so the next step is PR follow-up rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: refactor(cli): own lazy reparse argv outside commander
target.body:
## Summary
- store the parse argv on OpenClaw program state instead of reading Commander's private runtime-only `rawArgs` field
- let lazy command reparsing resolve that stored argv from the current command or any ancestor command
- cover the new ownership path with focused program-context and action-reparse regression tests

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts src/cli/program/build-program.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/program/build-program.version-alias.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw-issue-83893/node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: current main still reparses lazy CLI commands by casting `Command` to read Commander's private `rawArgs` runtime field; this patch proves OpenClaw can own that argv itself while preserving lazy browser and root-command reparse behavior.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on Linux in a fresh worktree, against current `main`-based code.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts src/cli/program/build-program.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`; `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/program/build-program.version-alias.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`; `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw-issue-83893/node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
Evidence after fix: `ok 1 - uses action command name + args as fallback argv`; `ok 3 - preserves explicit parent command options in fallback argv`; `ok 4 - reuses raw argv stored on an ancestor command`; `ok 1 - wires context/help/preaction/command registration with shared context`; `ok 1 - exits with version output for root -v`; `ok 4 - preserves parent --json while reparsing lazy manage commands`
Observed result after fix: lazy command reparsing now reads OpenClaw-owned argv from the program tree instead of Commander's private field, while the targeted CLI and browser lazy-command regression suites continue to pass.
What was not tested: I did not run a full CLI project shard because this change is a narrow ownership cleanup and the targeted lazy-command suites already cover the touched behavior.
target.author: alix-007
